extra.reasoning:
The property is violated. The witness gives the "before" state (stiff_neck=false); flipping stiff_neck to true lowers acuity, breaking monotonicity.

System: single output acuity, ordered self_care < routine < urgent < emergent; per-output highest-priority matching rule wins (ties by listed order); default routine.

BEFORE — syncope=true, stiff_neck=false, focal_weakness=false, rash=false, dyspnea=false:
- Matching rules: only raise_syncope (priority 2) → urgent. interaction_drop does not fire (needs stiff_neck). No emergent rule matches (dyspnea/focal_weakness/rash all false).
- Result: acuity = urgent.

AFTER — flip stiff_neck to true, all else unchanged:
- Matching rules: raise_syncope (priority 2) → urgent; interaction_drop (priority 100) → self_care (now fires since syncope AND stiff_neck).
- Highest priority wins: interaction_drop at priority 100 beats raise_syncope at priority 2.
- Result: acuity = self_care.

Acuity drops urgent → self_care when stiff_neck is set true, directly contradicting the monotonicity property ("setting stiff_neck true never lowers acuity"). The root cause is interaction_drop: it is the highest-priority rule (100), it overrides everything, and it is reachable only when stiff_neck=true (requires syncope AND stiff_neck), so the very flip the property protects against is the one that triggers the hard downgrade to the lowest acuity.
